local i = 1
repeat
  local x = i
  a[i] = function () i = x+1; return x end
until i > 10 or a[i]() ~= x
assert(i == 11 and a[1]() == 1 and a[3]() == 3 and i == 4)


-- testing closures created in 'then' and 'else' parts of 'if's
a = {}
for i = 1, 10 do
  if i % 3 == 0 then
    local y = 0
    a[i] = function (x) local t = y; y = x; return t end
  elseif i % 3 == 1 then
    goto L1
    error'not here'
  ::L1::
    local y = 1
    a[i] = function (x) local t = y; y = x; return t end
  elseif i % 3 == 2 then
    local t
    goto l4
    ::l4a:: a[i] = t; goto l4b
    error("should never be here!")
    ::l4::
    local y = 2
    t = function (x) local t = y; y = x; return t end
    goto l4a
    error("should never be here!")
    ::l4b::

-- $Id: testes/closure.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print "testing closures"

local A,B = 0,{g=10}
local function f(x)
  local a = {}
  for i=1,1000 do
    local y = 0
    do
      a[i] = function () B.g = B.g+1; y = y+x; return y+A end
    end
  end
  local dummy = function () return a[A] end
  collectgarbage()
  A = 1; assert(dummy() == a[1]); A = 0;
  assert(a[1]() == x)
  assert(a[3]() == x)
  collectgarbage()
  assert(B.g == 12)
  return a
end

local a = f(10)
-- force a GC in this level
local x = {[1] = {}}   -- to detect a GC
setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'})
while x[1] do   -- repeat until GC
  local a = A..A..A..A  -- create garbage
  A = A+1
end
assert(a[1]() == 20+A)
assert(a[1]() == 30+A)
assert(a[2]() == 10+A)
collectgarbage()
assert(a[2]() == 20+A)
assert(a[2]() == 30+A)
assert(a[3]() == 20+A)
assert(a[8]() == 10+A)
assert(getmetatable(x).__mode == 'kv')
assert(B.g == 19)


-- testing equality
a = {}

for i = 1, 5 do  a[i] = function (x) return i + a + _ENV end  end
assert(a[3] ~= a[4] and a[4] ~= a[5])

do
  local a = function (x)  return math.sin(_ENV[x])  end
  local function f()
    return a
  end
  assert(f() == f())
end


-- testing closures with 'for' control variable
a = {}
for i=1,10 do
  a[i] = {set = function(x) i=x end, get = function () return i end}
  if i == 3 then break end
end
assert(a[4] == undef)
a[1].set(10)
assert(a[2].get() == 2)
a[2].set('a')
assert(a[3].get() == 3)
assert(a[2].get() == 'a')

a = {}
local t = {"a", "b"}
for i = 1, #t do
  local k = t[i]
  a[i] = {set = function(x, y) i=x; k=y end,
          get = function () return i, k end}
  if i == 2 then break end
end
a[1].set(10, 20)
local r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == 2 and s == 'b')
r,s = a[1].get()
assert(r == 10 and s == 20)
a[2].set('a', 'b')
r,s = a[2].get()
assert(r == "a" and s == "b")


-- testing closures with 'for' control variable x break
local f
for i=1,3 do
  f = function () return i end
  break
end
assert(f() == 1)

for k = 1, #t do
  local v = t[k]
  f = function () return k, v end
  break
end
assert(({f()})[1] == 1)
assert(({f()})[2] == "a")


-- testing closure x break x return x errors

local b
function f(x)
  local first = 1
  while 1 do
    if x == 3 and not first then return end
    local a = 'xuxu'
    b = function (op, y)
          if op == 'set' then
            a = x+y
          else
            return a
          end
        end
    if x == 1 then do break end
    elseif x == 2 then return
    else if x ~= 3 then error() end
    end
    first = nil
  end
end

for i=1,3 do
  f(i)
  assert(b('get') == 'xuxu')
  b('set', 10); assert(b('get') == 10+i)
  b = nil
end

pcall(f, 4);
assert(b('get') == 'xuxu')
b('set', 10); assert(b('get') == 14)


local y, w
-- testing multi-level closure
function f(x)
  return function (y)
    return function (z) return w+x+y+z end
  end
end

y = f(10)
w = 1.345
assert(y(20)(30) == 60+w)


-- testing closures x break
do
  local X, Y
  local a = math.sin(0)

  while a do
    local b = 10
    X = function () return b end   -- closure with upvalue
    if a then break end
  end
  
  do
    local b = 20
    Y = function () return b end   -- closure with upvalue
  end

  -- upvalues must be different
  assert(X() == 10 and Y() == 20)
end

  
-- testing closures x repeat-until

local a = {}
local i = 1
repeat
  local x = i
  a[i] = function () i = x+1; return x end
until i > 10 or a[i]() ~= x
assert(i == 11 and a[1]() == 1 and a[3]() == 3 and i == 4)


-- testing closures created in 'then' and 'else' parts of 'if's
a = {}
for i = 1, 10 do
  if i % 3 == 0 then
    local y = 0
    a[i] = function (x) local t = y; y = x; return t end
  elseif i % 3 == 1 then
    goto L1
    error'not here'
  ::L1::
    local y = 1
    a[i] = function (x) local t = y; y = x; return t end
  elseif i % 3 == 2 then

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

delta.text: ib.rs:3845` had a `TODO(port)` stub: when the scope block inside `repeat...
until` captured upvalues, the codegen omitted the per-iteration `OP_CLOSE` and the brac
keting jumps the C parser emits. As a result, closures created in successive iterations shared the same upvalue slot inst
ead of getting fresh per-iteration captures — so the test's `local x = i
` was the same `x` across iterations, breaking the `a[1]() == 1

and a[3]() == 3` assertion at closure.lua:185.

**Fix:** Implemented the missing bran
it jump, patch `condexit` to the next pc, emit `OP_CLOSE reg_
level(fs, bl2.nactvar)`, emit a new jump (becomes the

replacement `condexit`), and patch the exit jump to here.

**New state:** Test now passes line 185 and prog
resses ~75 lines further (next failure at closure.lua:259 — a separate unrelated bug). No temporary inst
rumentation was added; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green.

content.text:
Clean build, no errors.

**Root cause:** `repeatstat` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3845` had a `TODO(port)` stub: when the scope block inside `repeat...until` captured upvalues, the codegen omitted the per-iteration `OP_CLOSE` and the bracketing jumps the C parser emits. As a result, closures created in successive iterations shared the same upvalue slot instead of getting fresh per-iteration captures — so the test's `local x = i` was the same `x` across iterations, breaking the `a[1]() == 1 and a[3]() == 3` assertion at closure.lua:185.

**Fix:** Implemented the missing branch (mirroring `lparser.c:1500-1506`): emit an exit jump, patch `condexit` to the next pc, emit `OP_CLOSE reg_level(fs, bl2.nactvar)`, emit a new jump (becomes the replacement `condexit`), and patch the exit jump to here.

**New state:** Test now passes line 185 and progresses ~75 lines further (next failure at closure.lua:259 — a separate unrelated bug). No temporary instrumentation was added; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green.

result:
Clean build, no errors.

**Root cause:** `repeatstat` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:3845` had a `TODO(port)` stub: when the scope block inside `repeat...until` captured upvalues, the codegen omitted the per-iteration `OP_CLOSE` and the bracketing jumps the C parser emits. As a result, closures created in successive iterations shared the same upvalue slot instead of getting fresh per-iteration captures — so the test's `local x = i` was the same `x` across iterations, breaking the `a[1]() == 1 and a[3]() == 3` assertion at closure.lua:185.

**Fix:** Implemented the missing branch (mirroring `lparser.c:1500-1506`): emit an exit jump, patch `condexit` to the next pc, emit `OP_CLOSE reg_level(fs, bl2.nactvar)`, emit a new jump (becomes the replacement `condexit`), and patch the exit jump to here.

**New state:** Test now passes line 185 and progresses ~75 lines further (next failure at closure.lua:259 — a separate unrelated bug). No temporary instrumentation was added; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green.
